response: 389 is prime. It's not divisible by any prime numbers up to its square root (≈19.7).
reasoning_content: We need to check if 389 is prime. Quick sanity check: it's not even, not divisible by 3 (3+8+9=20, not divisible by 3), not divisible by 5 (doesn't end in 0 or 5). Check divisibility by 7: 7*55=385, remainder 4, so not divisible. 11: 11*35=385, remainder 4, not divisible. 13: 13*30=390, remainder -1, so 389 = 13*29.92? Actually 13*29=377, 13*30=390, so no. 17: 17*22=374, 17*23=391, so no. 19: 19*20=380, 19*21=399, so no. 23: 23*16=368, 23*17=391, so no. 29: 29*13=377, 29*14=406, so no. 31: 31*12=372, 31*13=403, so no. 37: 37*10=370, 37*11=407, so no. 41: 41*9=369, 41*10=410, so no. 43: 43*9=387, remainder 2, so not. 47: 47*8=376, 47*9=423, so no. 53: 53*7=371, 53*8=424, so no. We only need to check up to sqrt(389) which is about 19.7, so up to 19. We've checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. So 389 is prime. Quick sanity: yes, it's prime.
